text: Yet further belabouring the point, I'm thinking that modern PLs sitting on top of bloated OSes (Linux ~40M LOC, Windows ~50-80M, macOS ~80-86M) suggests we've had code bloat baked in from the start — and while alternatives exist (MicroPython, Forth, etc.), continuing to stretch the current OS-dependent programming model to fit non-HPC problem domains is like adding epicycles.

elements.text: Yet further belabouring the point, I'm thinking that modern PLs sitting on top of bloated OSes (Linux ~40M LOC, Windows ~50-80M, macOS ~80-86M) suggests we've had code bloat baked in from the start — and while alternatives exist (MicroPython, Forth, etc.), continuing to stretch the current OS-dependent programming model to fit non-HPC problem domains is like adding epicycles.
